creator: girgen@FreeBSD.org
text:
on the jail host:

lo0: flags=1008049<UP,LOOPBACK,RUNNING,MULTICAST,LOWER_UP> metric 0 mtu 16384
	options=680003<RXCSUM,TXCSUM,LINKSTATE,RXCSUM_IPV6,TXCSUM_IPV6>
	inet 127.0.0.1 netmask 0xff000000
	inet6 ::1 prefixlen 128
	inet6 fe80::1%lo0 prefixlen 64 scopeid 0x1
	groups: lo
	nd6 options=21<PERFORMNUD,AUTO_LINKLOCAL>
pflog0: flags=1000141<UP,RUNNING,PROMISC,LOWER_UP> metric 0 mtu 33152
	options=0
	groups: pflog
bnxt0: flags=1008943<UP,BROADCAST,RUNNING,PROMISC,SIMPLEX,MULTICAST,LOWER_UP> metric 0 mtu 1500
	options=4e527bb<RXCSUM,TXCSUM,VLAN_MTU,VLAN_HWTAGGING,JUMBO_MTU,VLAN_HWCSUM,TSO4,TSO6,LRO,WOL_MAGIC,VLAN_HWFILTER,VLAN_HWTSO,RXCSUM_IPV6,TXCSUM_IPV6,HWSTATS,MEXTPG>
	ether 3c:ec:ef:9e:85:24
	media: Ethernet autoselect (1000baseT <full-duplex,rxpause,txpause>)
	status: active
	nd6 options=29<PERFORMNUD,IFDISABLED,AUTO_LINKLOCAL>
bnxt1: flags=1008943<UP,BROADCAST,RUNNING,PROMISC,SIMPLEX,MULTICAST,LOWER_UP> metric 0 mtu 1500
	options=4e527bb<RXCSUM,TXCSUM,VLAN_MTU,VLAN_HWTAGGING,JUMBO_MTU,VLAN_HWCSUM,TSO4,TSO6,LRO,WOL_MAGIC,VLAN_HWFILTER,VLAN_HWTSO,RXCSUM_IPV6,TXCSUM_IPV6,HWSTATS,MEXTPG>
	ether 3c:ec:ef:9e:85:25
	inet 192.168.1.79 netmask 0xffffff00 broadcast 192.168.1.255
	media: Ethernet autoselect (1000baseT <full-duplex,rxpause,txpause>)
	status: active
	nd6 options=29<PERFORMNUD,IFDISABLED,AUTO_LINKLOCAL>
ue0: flags=8802<BROADCAST,SIMPLEX,MULTICAST> metric 0 mtu 1500
	options=0
	ether be:3a:f2:b6:05:9f
	nd6 options=29<PERFORMNUD,IFDISABLED,AUTO_LINKLOCAL>


# ngctl list
There are 10 total nodes:
  Name: ng0_test2jail   Type: eiface          ID: 00000fe0   Num hooks: 1
  Name: bnxt0           Type: ether           ID: 00000001   Num hooks: 2
  Name: bnxt1           Type: ether           ID: 00000002   Num hooks: 2
  Name: ue0             Type: ether           ID: 00000003   Num hooks: 0
  Name: bnxt0bridge     Type: bridge          ID: 00000009   Num hooks: 4
  Name: ng1_test2jail   Type: eiface          ID: 00000fea   Num hooks: 1
  Name: ng0_testjail    Type: eiface          ID: 00000fca   Num hooks: 1
  Name: ngctl22294      Type: socket          ID: 00000fed   Num hooks: 0
  Name: ng1_testjail    Type: eiface          ID: 00000fd3   Num hooks: 1
  Name: bnxt1bridge     Type: bridge          ID: 00000016   Num hooks: 4


inside the jail test2jail

lo0: flags=1008049<UP,LOOPBACK,RUNNING,MULTICAST,LOWER_UP> metric 0 mtu 16384
        options=680003<RXCSUM,TXCSUM,LINKSTATE,RXCSUM_IPV6,TXCSUM_IPV6>
        inet 127.0.0.1 netmask 0xff000000
        inet6 ::1 prefixlen 128
        inet6 fe80::1%lo0 prefixlen 64 scopeid 0xc
        groups: lo
        nd6 options=21<PERFORMNUD,AUTO_LINKLOCAL>
pflog0: flags=0 metric 0 mtu 33152
        options=0
        groups: pflog
ng0_test2jail: flags=1008842<BROADCAST,RUNNING,SIMPLEX,MULTICAST,LOWER_UP> metric 0 mtu 1500
        options=28<VLAN_MTU,JUMBO_MTU>
        ether 02:20:c9:9e:85:24
        hwaddr 58:9c:fc:10:58:4e
        media: Ethernet autoselect (1000baseT <full-duplex>)
        status: active
        nd6 options=29<PERFORMNUD,IFDISABLED,AUTO_LINKLOCAL>
ng1_test2jail: flags=1008843<UP,BROADCAST,RUNNING,SIMPLEX,MULTICAST,LOWER_UP> metric 0 mtu 1500
        options=28<VLAN_MTU,JUMBO_MTU>
        ether 02:20:c9:9e:85:25
        hwaddr 58:9c:fc:00:16:65
        inet 192.168.1.197 netmask 0xffffff00 broadcast 192.168.1.255
        media: Ethernet autoselect (1000baseT <full-duplex>)
        status: active
        nd6 options=29<PERFORMNUD,IFDISABLED,AUTO_LINKLOCAL>




if this jail is now stopped. the host will crash reproducably. I've only ever seen this on one kind of hardware, brand new SuperMicro server with the bnxt interface. For all other hardware we use, it works just fine to have interfaces without and IP number and stop the jail... well most of the time. We still see sporadic crashes sadly, as mentioned, but no reproducable crash like this.
